text.title: SAFe Events: PI Planning, Scrum-of-Scrums, PO Sync, Inspect-and-Adapt
text.summary: Structure and rules for the four SAFe program-level events used in multi-team agile programs.

rule.statement: Lock the PI Planning agenda exactly; teams that improvise the structure lose 30 minutes per ceremony to confusion and shortchange team breakout time.
rule.rationale: PI Planning is the highest-value SAFe ceremony per hour. Agenda deviations compound across all teams simultaneously, creating schedule pressure that degrades plan quality.

rule.statement: Pre-build the program board the day before remote PI Planning; live-building the board during the ceremony kills momentum.
rule.rationale: Remote PI Planning with real-time board construction causes technical delays that disrupt the flow and reduce breakout time in which teams actually plan.

list.item: Day 1: business context (30 min), product vision (30 min), architecture vision (30 min), planning context (30 min), team breakouts (4 hrs), draft plan review (1 hr).
Day 2: planning adjustments (1 hr), team breakouts (2 hrs), final plan review (2 hrs), confidence vote (15 min), planning retrospective (30 min).
Outputs: committed PI objectives per team, program board with dependencies, identified risks, confidence vote recorded.

rule.statement: Focus Scrum-of-Scrums on cross-team dependency resolution, not status reporting; teams that turn it into a round-robin get no value from it.
rule.rationale: Status reporting belongs in individual team standups. Scrum-of-Scrums is the only forum specifically for inter-team blockers and integration issues — use it exclusively for that.

list.item: Frequency: daily or every other day.
Participants: one Scrum Master per team, Release Train Engineer as facilitator.
Questions: what did your team accomplish since last, what will they do before next, what impediments are they facing, what dependencies exist with other teams.
Participants: all Product Owners, Product Manager as facilitator.
Agenda: feature priorities (10 min), dependency coordination (10 min), roadmap updates (5 min), impediments (5 min).
Output: aligned priorities across teams, dependencies managed, roadmap updates communicated.

rule.statement: Track Inspect-and-Adapt action items in the same program backlog as features; items tracked separately get prioritized to zero and disappear.
rule.rationale: I&A actions without formal backlog entries have no owner or sprint assignment. They exist only in the retro notes, which no one reads between PIs.

list.item: Agenda: PI system demo (45 min), quantitative measurement review (30 min), retrospective workshop (90 min), action planning (15 min).

section.title: ScrumBan Hybrid
section.p: ScrumBan combines sprint planning for steering (from Scrum) with WIP limits and continuous flow (from Kanban). Timeboxing becomes flexible; commitment is to WIP capacity, not sprint content. Metrics: cycle time plus velocity reported together.

text.reference: Kanban metrics dashboard and PI Planning board scaffold
Throughput and cycle-time stats for flow-based forecasting
